title: Pair-Supervised Regularization for Selective Counterfactual Edits in Frozen Vision SAEs
description: Train a frozen-vision sparse autoencoder with pair supervision that keeps validated nuisance-preserving pairs stable while making exact single-factor counterfactual differences concentrated and directionally repeatable. The contribution is a narrow regularization study for counterfactual edit selectivity in one frozen vision backbone, not a broad advance in concept learning.
motivation: Recent 2025 vision-SAE papers already cover editable frozen vision features, dictionary stabilization, concept-grounded supervision, and stronger sparse-coding families. The remaining tractable gap is whether SAE training can be regularized directly on paired differences so that latent edits become more target-selective under a shared, fair evaluation pipeline.
proposed_approach: Use frozen DINOv2 ViT-S/14 activations and train a standard SAE with reconstruction, sparsity, audited nuisance-invariance, counterfactual-delta concentration, same-factor signed-delta alignment, and weak cross-factor separation losses. Evaluate on Shapes3D against vanilla SAE, Archetypal SAE, and a one-seed MP-SAE check under symmetric model selection and two shared edit rules: a sparse top-K association edit and a dense signed-centroid edit; treat any CelebA result as descriptive unless a strict pair-quality audit passes.

related_work.title: Interpretable and Testable Vision Features via Sparse Autoencoders
related_work.how_it_differs: This paper already shows standard vision SAEs can support causal editing, so the proposed work does not claim first editable vision features; it asks whether pair-supervised training improves edit selectivity under the same downstream editing setup.

related_work.title: Archetypal SAE: Adaptive and Stable Dictionary Learning for Concept Extraction in Large Vision Models
related_work.how_it_differs: Archetypal SAE regularizes global dictionary geometry and stability, whereas the proposed method regularizes how latent codes move under validated nuisance pairs and exact single-factor counterfactual pairs.

related_work.title: From Flat to Hierarchical: Extracting Sparse Representations with Matching Pursuit
related_work.how_it_differs: MP-SAE is a stronger sparse-coding family rather than a pair-supervised regularizer; it is included as a confirmatory baseline to test whether any gain survives against a stronger encoder.

related_work.title: Concept-SAE: Active Causal Probing of Visual Model Behavior
related_work.how_it_differs: Concept-SAE uses semantic supervision to learn grounded concept tokens for causal probing and localization, while the proposed method uses only pair labels to regularize concentrated and repeatable latent deltas.

related_work.title: Causal Interpretation of Sparse Autoencoder Features in Vision
related_work.how_it_differs: That paper improves causal explanation of existing vision-SAE features via patch interventions, whereas the proposed work changes SAE training to improve the selectivity of latent feature edits.

related_work.title: Projecting Assumptions: The Duality Between Sparse Autoencoders and Concept Geometry
related_work.how_it_differs: Provides a theoretical caution that SAE behavior depends on data geometry; the proposal responds by restricting its main claim to exact-factor synthetic counterfactuals rather than broad real-world concept discovery.

related_work.title: Sparse Autoencoders Do Not Find Canonical Units of Analysis
related_work.how_it_differs: Argues SAE units are not canonical, which motivates the proposal's focus on intervention selectivity under shared edit rules rather than claims that one neuron should equal one concept.

hypothesis: If a frozen vision SAE is trained so that audited nuisance-preserving pairs keep codes stable and exact single-factor counterfactual pairs induce concentrated, aligned signed latent differences, then under symmetric checkpoint selection and shared edit rules it will produce larger target-factor prediction shifts and smaller off-target changes than vanilla SAE and Archetypal SAE without materially harming reconstruction.

confirm: On Shapes3D, the pair-supervised SAE improves at least one primary intervention metric over both vanilla SAE and Archetypal SAE while maintaining equal or better off-target preservation.
The improvement is visible under both shared edit rules, or otherwise is reported explicitly as depending on the sparse edit rule.
Reconstruction remains within 5% relative of the best matched-budget baseline and the code does not collapse to trivial sparsity.
The advantage is not fully removed by the one-seed MP-SAE confirmatory baseline.
refute: Apparent gains vanish on downstream prediction-shift or counterfactual-consistency tests and survive only in proxy diagnostics.
Archetypal SAE or MP-SAE matches or exceeds the proposed method once model selection and edit construction are made symmetric.
The nuisance-pair audit fails, making the invariance supervision unreliable.
The observed gain depends entirely on the top-K edit rule or disappears outside the chosen token representation.
No non-synthetic result survives a strict pair-quality audit, in which case the proposal must be treated only as a synthetic mechanism study.
